place: Harvey Cedars borough, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: A 30‑foot tower nicknamed the “owl’s tree” rose above the highest dune, giving whalers a great view to spot the migrating right whale in February and March.
The Harvey Cedars Hotel, opened around 1850, became a sportsman’s boarding house known as “Kinsey’s” and was run by Civil War veteran John Warner Kinsey.
Late‑nineteenth‑century life in Harvey Cedars revolved around one of the country’s first lifesaving stations and the hotel that later became the Bible Conference.
Hotel owner Isaac Jennings was elected mayor, a rare example of a business leader taking on municipal office in the town’s early history.
The small, cedar‑shake covered High Point station once stood where the borough hall sits today, a reminder of the town’s rail past.
Harvey Cedars’ original train stop was a modest covered platform located just off Atlantic Avenue, the main thoroughfare through town.
